traceAssessment.assessment: Spent full 12-call budget on get_market for each sibling sub-leg; all came back finalized with terminal 0/100 prices. Response honestly reports the dead-end rather than fabricating EV rankings, but buyer still gets no actionable ranking. Event/market finalization is upstream reality, not a runtime bug.

perQueryEvaluations.autoPromoteReason: Paid grounds the refusal in real finalized settlements and real sub-leg titles; free baseline constructs a fictitious naive-prior table.

summary.notes: Iter-6 sub-market fallback for handleGetEventCandlesticks clearly lifted q10 from 3.33 -> 4.50 by materializing top-volume sub-market OHLC rows in-band; the response now cites real daily candles instead of claiming an empty event series. q22 correctly unaffected (market-level, not event-level). Remaining sub-4.0 is q12 only, which is an upstream crossref parlay-decomposition issue (string-match on raw parlay title returns unrelated Bolivian soccer matches) rather than a runtime candlestick path problem. Overall mean across the 7 rescored queries = 4.45, up from the iter-5 mean of 4.13 over the same seven prompts.
